name: Fundamental Constants of the Universe
description: The numbers that define reality. Speed of light, gravitational constant, Planck's constant, Boltzmann's constant. These are not chosen -- they are measured. They cannot be derived from each other. They are the axioms of physics. Hash them and see where the universe puts itself.
connection: Spectral Binding says addresses are discovered, not assigned. These constants were discovered, not assigned. Nobody chose c = 299,792,458 m/s. The universe chose it. Where does the universe's own address land?

constants.name: Speed of Light
constants.note: The speed limit. Nothing with mass can reach it.

constants.name: Gravitational Constant
constants.note: How strongly mass attracts mass. Weakest force.

constants.name: Planck Constant
constants.note: The minimum action. Reality is granular below this.

constants.name: Elementary Charge
constants.note: The charge of one proton. Quantized.

constants.name: Fine Structure Constant
constants.note: Strength of electromagnetic interaction. Dimensionless. If it were different, atoms wouldn't form.

constants.name: Proton Mass
constants.note: 1836 times heavier than electron. Nobody knows why.

constants.name: Avogadro Number
constants.note: Bridge between atomic and human scale.

constants.name: Gas Constant
constants.note: k_B times N_A. Macro version of Boltzmann.

constants.name: Stefan-Boltzmann Constant
constants.note: How fast hot things radiate. Stars obey this.

constants.name: Hubble Constant
constants.note: Rate of expansion of the universe. Still debated.

constants.name: Cosmological Constant
constants.note: Dark energy density. 70% of the universe is this and we don't know what it is.

constants.name: CMB Temperature
constants.note: Temperature of the universe. Afterglow of the Big Bang. Measured everywhere, same in every direction.

black_holes.name: Schwarzschild Radius
black_holes.note: The point of no return. Below this radius, escape velocity exceeds c. Space and time swap roles.

black_holes.name: Hawking Temperature
black_holes.note: Black holes radiate. Temperature is inversely proportional to mass. Small black holes are hot. Stellar ones are colder than the CMB.

black_holes.name: Bekenstein-Hawking Entropy
black_holes.note: Entropy is proportional to surface area, not volume. Information lives on the boundary. This is the holographic principle.

black_holes.name: Chandrasekhar Limit
black_holes.note: Above this mass, electron degeneracy pressure fails. The star collapses. White dwarf becomes neutron star or black hole.

exercise: Hash each constant name to hex. Resolve through chip_resolve_hex. Do the cosmological constants cluster? Do the quantum ones? Find the midpoint between Speed of Light and Gravitational Constant -- the two forces that define spacetime. What element band is the bridge between light and gravity?
